title: Introduction to React
content:
### What is React?

React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces, maintained by Meta. It enables developers to create single-page applications (SPAs) with reusable components and a declarative approach to UI development.

---

### Key Concepts

| Concept | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| Components | Reusable, self-contained pieces of UI |
| Virtual DOM | Lightweight in-memory representation of the real DOM |
| Declarative | Describe what the UI should look like, React handles updates |
| Unidirectional Data Flow | Data flows from parent to child via props |

### Single-Page Applications (SPA)

In a traditional multi-page app, each navigation triggers a full page reload. SPAs load a single HTML page and dynamically update content using JavaScript. React excels at building SPAs because it efficiently updates only the parts of the DOM that change.

### Virtual DOM

When state changes, React creates a new virtual DOM tree, compares it with the previous one (diffing), and applies only the necessary real DOM updates (reconciliation). This minimizes expensive DOM operations.

> **Key Point:** React is a library, not a framework. It focuses on the view layer and integrates with other libraries for routing, state management, and data fetching.

quiz.question: What is React primarily used for?
What is the Virtual DOM?
options: A direct copy of the real DOM
A lightweight in-memory representation of the DOM
A database for storing DOM elements
A browser API for DOM manipulation
